title: Seed Registry, Origin Scope, Budgets, and Stop Policy · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in deny-by-default crawl-scope policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Bind each seed to owner authorization, purpose, expected content class, policy version, contact, and expiry.
Define allowed schemes, hosts, ports, subdomains, redirects, and cross-origin resources with deny-by-default admission.
Specify methods, path prefixes, query keys and values, fragments, pagination, downloads, forms, and content types as testable rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Seed Registry
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 3390 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to admit authorized seeds through origin, path, query, multiresource budget, stop, and contact policy; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one duplicate, accessibility state, axe result, resource budget, browser readiness, or manual-review condition without overclaiming. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bind each seed to owner authorization, purpose, expected content class, policy version, contact, and expiry.

Inspect the Seed Registry trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Seed Registry.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bind each seed to owner authorization, purpose, expected content class, policy version, contact, and expiry. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Seed Registry, keep Seed Registry connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: For Seed Registry, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

stimulus.title: Seed Registry case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A debug TypeScript team handles case 3390 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. lines.label: risk
lines.text: A hostname alone is a sufficient seed and audit identity.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind each seed to owner authorization, purpose, expected content class, policy version, contact, and expiry. Artifact under review: deny-by-default crawl-scope policy.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Seed Registry, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Seed Registry record says deny-by-default crawl-scope policy looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Seed Registry.

For inspect the failure record, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof. This does not satisfy Seed Registry for deny-by-default crawl-scope policy.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Stop Contact
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-stop-contact-23hzcf" that uses case 8937 to admit authorized seeds through origin, path, query, multiresource budget, stop, and contact policy. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Stop Contact.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Stop Contact into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Stop Contact, keep Stop Contact connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Stop Contact structural check is only one gate. deny-by-default crawl-scope policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Implement automatic stop triggers, owner pause, kill path, partial-output policy, cleanup, incident contact, and restart authorization.
